title: Field Research Centre
text:
The Field Research Centre (FRC) is the ecological field station of Hasselt University, located at the gateway to Nationaal Park Hoge Kempen. We conduct international research on the impacts of human activities on ecology and biodiversity.
Explore Our Research Meet the Team





Part of the European eLTER network, collecting data across Nationaal Park Hoge Kempen using camera traps and biologgers.



Investigating how human density, recreation, and habitat fragmentation impact wildlife dynamics and biodiversity in complex, multi-use landscapes.



Developing predictive models to evaluate management strategies and support evidence-based wildlife management and conservation.

title: Imke Tomsin
text: My research focuses on the development and application of mechanistic models to support decision-making in wildlife management and conservation. I am particularly interested in human-wildlife interactions within social-ecological systems, with a focus on mammals including roe deer, wild boar, and wolves, and previously the European hamster.

title: Imke Tomsin

title: Imke Tomsin
text:
Research Interests

Individual-/agent-based modelling
(Generalised) Management Strategy Evaluation ((G)MSE)
Human-wildlife interactions & social-ecological systems
Wildlife management & conservation
Good Modelling Practices & open science
Mammals

title: Field Research Centre
text:
Part of the European eLTER network, collecting data across Nationaal Park Hoge Kempen using camera traps and biologgers.



Investigating how human density, recreation, and habitat fragmentation impact wildlife dynamics and biodiversity in complex, multi-use landscapes.

title: Field Research Centre
text:
Note📍 Located at the Gateway to National Park Hoge Kempen



Our unique location provides direct access to heathland, pine forests, and restored ecosystems, serving as a living laboratory for national and international research collaborations.

title: Field Research Centre
text:
New Publication: Check out our team’s latest research on social-ecological systems and wildlife management.
